name: Empanaditas
recipeIngredient: 1/4 cup Cottage cheese; sm curd, cream
recipeInstructions:
1. Hard boiled egg should be peeled and chopped.
2. Cook the ground beef in a 10-inch skillet until brown, breaking it up into small pieces. Drain, reserving 1 tablespoon of fat.
3. Stir in onion, raisins, olives, salt, and pepper. Cover and cook over low heat for 5 minutes.
4. Stir in cottage cheese and chopped hard boiled egg.
5. Preheat oven to 400 degrees F (200 degrees C).
6. Mix egg white and water until slightly foamy; reserve.
7. Prepare pastry dough; gather into a ball. Divide into halves.
8. Shape each half into flattened rounds on a lightly floured surface.
9. Roll one round of pasty into a circle, about 14 inches in diameter. Cut into 11 or 12 circles, 3.5 inches in diameter.
10. Spoon 2 tablespoons of the beef mixture onto the center of each circle. Brush the edge of the pastry with egg white mixture.
11. Fold the pastry circle over the filling; press the edge with a fork to seal.
12. Place empanaditas on an ungreased cookie sheet. Repeat with remaining pastry circles.
13. Gather any remaining pastry; shape into another round. Roll, cut, and fill as before.
14. Beat egg yolk and milk until well blended; brush over tops of empanaditas.
15. Bake until golden brown, 15 to 20 minutes. Serve warm.
